sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_time (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, ts TIMESTAMP, vals LIST<INTEGER>);
SELECT id, epoch_ns(ts) AS epoch_nano FROM test_time WHERE vals @> [1, 2] AND NOT ts IS NULL;
SELECT id, epoch_ns(ts) AS epoch_nano FROM test_time WHERE vals @> (SELECT [3, 4] WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1));
SELECT id, epoch_ns(ts) AS epoch_nano FROM test_time WHERE vals @> (SELECT DISTINCT UNNEST(vals) FROM test_time WHERE id = 1) AND ts >= timestamp '2021-01-01';
SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS count FROM test_time GROUP BY id HAVING epoch_ns(ts) > 0 AND vals @> [5];
SELECT id, vals, epoch_ns(ts) FROM test_time WHERE vals IS NOT NULL UNION SELECT 4, [6, 7, 8], epoch_ns(ts) FROM test_time WHERE ts IS NULL;
SELECT id, vals FROM test_time ORDER BY epoch_ns(ts) DESC LIMIT 1;
WITH temp AS (SELECT epoch_ns(ts) AS epoch_time, id FROM test_time) SELECT id FROM temp WHERE epoch_time > (SELECT MAX(epoch_time) FROM temp);
